config.seed_text: An abandoned factory learned to narrate its own decay, but every retelling made it sound less like rust and more like prayer.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text:
It's not so much because one had been trained as "you're lazy" or whatever; rather, all of you were going through an awful lot while doing what should be easy for everyone else (although even at some level this was becoming apparent).

 [Translator Notes] I think we can agree on something here from The Book Of Life itself -- people will tell us how they felt about learning new words by listening to them themselves before their time has expired when someone talks/plays with each other via screencasts , which is actually very good stuff! And if anyone ever asks me whether there might also have happened similar instances where characters said different types than others did throughout most of my childhoods... well then why don't these examples illustrate exactly WHY? So far everything seemed almost pointless until finally getting
records.output_text: out into public service again ;-) Thanks BAMFULLY!!!!!

records.reflection_text: I think it might be interesting to rework some of your words by changing them from "The word 'I' is usually used when you're saying, or at least responding with, as an actual noun" to "the concept (as defined) often comes first." If so then this would allow us... *to go back through all existing sentences which contained any hint about how they were actually being said*. And if there's even one thing here we need! That kind body of thinking could let other people come up ...
